programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: Melissa Mackay presents the latest from ABC News on the weekend bringing you today's top stories and coverage of events as they unfold. Plus original reporting, analysis and updated news, sport, weather and information.

programme.title: Blue Murder Motel
programme.desc: A summer market is the scene of a freak accident, resulting in the death of a local candy merchant. Vinny and Cole take a closer look and discover that the murder may be more personal than either of them imagined. (Final).

programme.title: Patience
programme.desc: Crime drama. Resentment boils over between Patience and Frankie as they investigate the death of a botanist. And Jake takes Patience speed-dating.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the extraordinary riches of Galapagos, beginning with the story of the birth of the islands and how it became populated with life.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: David descends beneath the waves in a state-of-the art submersible, the first of its kind to visit the reef. He meets some of the tiny coral animals who built the reef, creating an underwater wonderland.

programme.title: Judgment: Cases That Changed Australia
programme.desc: When asylum seekers' futures are argued before the High Court, questions of borders, power and national identity are laid bare.

programme.title: Australian Story
programme.sub-title: Dog Gone - The Hunt for Valerie
programme.desc: How the hunt for runaway sausage dog Valerie fired up the Kangaroo Island community and sparked headlines around the world.

programme.title: Landline
programme.desc: A tasty sampler of stories from the land - covering food, agriculture, crops, climate, business and innovation - as we meet the fascinating people who make our rural and regional communities unique. Presented by Pip Courtney.

programme.title: Kath & Kim
programme.desc: A trip to Ikea leads Kim to doubt Brett's manliness. Stressed over her final project for a floral design course, Kath takes one too many \.

programme.title: Hard Quiz
programme.desc: The Big Brass Mug is up for grabs again as Tom Gleeson quizzes another quartet on their questionable topics: the musical Rent, Grammy-winner Beyonce, Australian finches, and Aussie TV classic SeaChange.

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: News from where we live. ABC News Victoria brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to Victorians. Presented by Iskhandar Razak, plus sport with Alexia Pesce.

programme.title: Death In Paradise
programme.desc: We’re back on the beautiful island of Saint Marie, and this time with a new detective inspector - Londoner Mervin Wilson - whose first case is to work out who shot Santa.

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Harriet, the new boss, arrives and puts the team on edge. Meanwhile, the mummified body of a woman is discovered in a cave, her fingers having been burned with acid to disguise her identity.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: On the infamous Swanage field trip, Jay is on the lookout for a Swanage MILF reported to have a thing for young boys. And Neil is desperately trying to deflect the advances of the overly friendly Mr Kennedy.

programme.title: rage Guest Programmer: Melanie C
programme.desc: Australia's longest running music TV show, rage is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres and eras. Guest programmers curate an all-night playlist of their all-time favourite clips.

programme.title: rage Charts
programme.desc: Keep the party vibe going as the sun comes up with continuous music videos, from new releases to classics and the songs we've long forgotten.

programme.title: rage Charts
programme.desc: Keep the party vibe going with wall-to-wall music videos, from new releases to classics and the long-forgotten.

programme.title: Weekend Breakfast
programme.desc: All the news you need to start your Sunday. Fauziah Ibrahim and Johanna Nicholson bring you the latest news, interviews, politics, culture, weather and global affairs. Plus sport with Daniela Intili.

programme.title: Insiders
programme.desc: Just days out from the Farrer by-election, David Speers and the panel discuss the campaign and the mood of the electorate in this crucial test. Plus the state of the economy ahead of an interest rate decision and the budget.

programme.title: Offsiders
programme.desc: For 20 years, Offsiders has been here to unpack the week in sport. Abbey Gelmi and the panel discuss the latest AFL, NRL, Women's Rugby League State of Origin, Men's and Women's A-League Finals, Super Rugby and Super Netball.

programme.title: The World This Week
programme.desc: Girish Sawlani brings you a weekly roundup of international news from an Australian perspective as reported by ABC News correspondents covering the Indo Pacific, Europe, North America, the Middle East, India and Indonesia.

programme.title: Compass
programme.desc: A descendant of First Nations WWI soldier William Allan Irwin sets out on an emotional journey to France, where he seeks to honour his ancestor and return his spirit home to Gomeroi country. A Compass Anzac Day special.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Fern Britton joins Sean Fletcher on the Cornish coast to reflect on her journey of faith.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: Melissa Mackay presents the latest from ABC News on the weekend bringing you today's top stories and coverage of events as they unfold. Plus original reporting, analysis and updated news, sport, weather and information.

programme.title: Landline
programme.desc: Hosted by award-winning journalist Pip Courtney, Landline is Australia's only national agricultural television program covering stories from Australia's rural and regional heartland.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.sub-title: Autumn: Rare Fruit Collection & Beverly Kills
programme.desc: Costa explores native plants for urban gardens; Josh uncovers a rare fruit collection; Hannah visits a colourful cottage garden; Millie celebrates the humble spud.

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: The Assembly's previous graduate students return and are mentored by Leigh Sales as they prepare and interview comedian, book author and renowned radio and TV personality, Andy Lee.

programme.title: Judgment: Cases That Changed Australia
programme.desc: When asylum seekers' futures are argued before the High Court, questions of borders, power and national identity are laid bare.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kathryn plans an accessible, eco-friendly home next to her beloved Edwardian house, determined to stay on her cherished North London street.

programme.title: Back Roads
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part special, Back Roads guest presenter Rae Johnston tackles the iconic Binns Track through the red heart of Central Australia.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Roadshow is at Pitzhanger Manor in Ealing, West London, where finds include an 18th century 'Harlequinade' children's flip book, vintage trainers, and a flying helmet from the Battle of Britain.

programme.title: Compass
programme.desc: Ukrainian vet Maria Mykytiuk lived an idyllic life until Russia invaded Bucha in 2022. She survived violence and death of her best friend. Now safe in Australia, she uses her voice to help Ukraine.

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: News from where we live. ABC News Queensland brings you today's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting. Presented by Jessica van Vonderen, sport with Mackenzie Colahan, plus weather with Jacqui McLaren.

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: Renowned interviewer, Leigh Sales mentors autistic journalism students in The Assembly. In this episode, celebrated actor and producer Claudia Karvan shares insights into her work and relationships.

programme.title: Bad Company
programme.desc: Julia is determined to get The Argyle on track with performance reviews, creating chaos as Margie's new play is about to open. Meanwhile Julia's son Ryan wanders the halls, catching Margie's eye.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: It's work experience week, and there's been a mix-up, meaning that Neil's at a paper and Will is at a garage. Mr Gilbert is unwilling to change things, so Will gets to see a place where he's \.

programme.title: Death In Paradise
programme.desc: We’re back on the beautiful island of Saint Marie, and this time with a new detective inspector - Londoner Mervin Wilson - whose first case is to work out who shot Santa.

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Harriet, the new boss, arrives and puts the team on edge. Meanwhile, the mummified body of a woman is discovered in a cave, her fingers having been burned with acid to disguise her identity.

programme.title: Rage Vault
programme.desc: From Bon Jovi to Iron Maiden, Poison, David Lee Roth, Siouxsie and The Banshees and more, rage dives into the Vault to bring you the best rock and metal clips from 1986.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.sub-title: Planting Trees, Native Climbers & Kiwi Prune
programme.desc: Costa meets city gardeners who are taking it to the street, Millie shows how to plant mature trees, Josh explores a protea paradise, and Jane finds a garden fence that's as pretty as a picture.

programme.title: Dream Gardens
programme.desc: A family rebuild their entire home to make way for an incredible Australian bush billabong retreat in the heart of the city. But can this radical garden really thrive in an urban environment?

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: There's no 'fowl' play in Alice's kitchen today, when comedian Rhys Nicholson pops past and they put a nutritious spin on fried chicken with Alice.

programme.title: Insiders
programme.desc: Just days out from the Farrer by-election, David Speers and the panel discuss the campaign and the mood of the electorate in this crucial test. Plus the state of the economy ahead of an interest rate decision and the budget.

programme.title: News Breakfast (Sydney)
programme.desc: James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, overnight developments, interviews and new details on the top stories this morning, joined by Catherine Murphy with sport and Lillian Rangiah with weather.

programme.title: ABC News Mornings
programme.desc: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Harriet, the new boss, arrives and puts the team on edge. Meanwhile, the mummified body of a woman is discovered in a cave, her fingers having been burned with acid to disguise her identity.

programme.title: You Can't Ask That
programme.desc: Setting the record straight about what it's really like to be short statured, find out the answers to the questions Australians wanted to ask but were too embarrassed or afraid to ever ask in person.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: The leading news at lunchtime. Kathryn Robinson brings you today's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter.

programme.title: Landline
programme.desc: Hosted by award-winning journalist Pip Courtney, Landline is Australia's only national agricultural television program covering stories from Australia's rural and regional heartland.
